crossCuttingThemes: Green-for-directional-intent is the dominant residual violation: buy/call/bullish/long still renders green (instead of blue) in 15+ spots across the header broadcast stack, FlowScreen spread column, trade strategy chips, algo KPI row labels, MarketDemoScreen hero chips, and six independent helpers inside ResearchChartSurface.tsx — a single semanticToneModel (toneForDirectionalIntent/toneForOptionSide) sweep per file closes all of them.

Hand-rolled SegmentedControl clones are the most widespread primitive duplication (~15 sites across Flow, Trade, Backtesting, Research, Diagnostics, Settings, Account, TickerSearch): mapped <button> rows with manual active styling, often missing radiogroup/aria-pressed semantics that the primitive provides for free.

Hand-rolled StatTile/MetricChip stat tiles recur in 10+ files (Flow, GEX, Signals, Account, Diagnostics MetricCard, Settings status strip, MobileMoreSheet, PositionRowActionMenu) — StatTile's docstring already names these dialects as consolidation targets.

Bare empty/loading states instead of DataUnavailableState/Skeleton appear in 15+ branches (DiagnosticsScreen alone has 6), plus text/emoji spinners in ResearchCalendarView — empty states consistently lack context and recovery affordances.

Status indicators (dot+label pills) are hand-rolled instead of StatusPill in Diagnostics, PlatformShell footer, FooterMemoryPressureIndicator, Settings SourceBadge, and AccountScreen transition status.
Green misused as a generic selection/active highlight (DiagnosticsScreen tabs+range, SettingsScreen smallButton incl. the dirty Save button) — selection should be the accent token.

Not-configured operational states collapse to RED instead of AMBER (PlatformShell FooterStatusField, MobileMoreSheet IBKR chip), and conversely hard failures render AMBER instead of RED in SettingsScreen banners — the 3-state operational scale is being flattened from both ends.
Raw hex/rgba/shadow literals bypass tokens (AlgoSaveBar and ChartTickerSearch shadows vs ELEVATION, MachineStateDiagram #e8a13a vs CSS_COLOR.amber, PlatformShell #F7FAFF fallback).
Emoji and bare glyphs as decoration/status without the Icon primitive or aria-labels (★ golden markers, 🔔 alert button, ⚡ 0DTE, 📄/📅 tab labels, 🔑/📅 empty-state ornaments, decorative FlaskConical).
Clickable divs/rows without keyboard access concentrated in the Research area (PhotonicsObservatory peer/supplier/graph/heatmap cells, ResearchCalendarView earnings rows).

Unguarded infinite animations ignore prefers-reduced-motion (PhotonicsObservatory edgeFlow, TradeChainPanel's two inline keyframe spinners) because inline keyframes escape the class-scoped reduced-motion CSS.

rawConfirmed.issue: The hero 'Net flow' MetricChip (value = call premium − put premium) is toned with toneForFinancialDelta(flow.net), so net call inflow (bullish) renders GREEN via the live MetricChip tone prop. toneForFinancialDelta returns SEMANTIC_TONE.financialPositive (CSS_COLOR.green) for positive values. Net premium flow is directional market intent, and its production siblings render it BLUE (MarketScreen sector flow via toneForMarketPressure at MarketScreen.jsx:595; MarketInternalsRail sector-net via toneForDirectionalIntent).
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: SEMANTIC COLOR — directional market intent: BLUE for buy/call/bullish/long/inflow; green is BANNED for directional intent.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Replace toneForFinancialDelta(flow.net) with toneForDirectionalIntent (SEMANTIC_TONE.directionBuy blue / directionSell red) or a toneForMarketPressure-style helper, matching MarketScreen.

rawConfirmed.issue: The hero 'P/C' MetricChip is toned with toneForFinancialDelta(1 - flow.putCall), and flow.putCall = putPrem/callPrem, so a call-lead (putCall < 1 = bullish) ratio yields a positive delta → GREEN via the live MetricChip tone prop. Put/Call positioning bias is directional sentiment; the production MarketScreen tones the same Put/Call metric with MARKET_BULLISH_TONE/MARKET_BEARISH_TONE (blue/red) at MarketScreen.jsx:579.
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: SEMANTIC COLOR — directional market intent: BLUE for bullish/call-side; green is BANNED for directional intent.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Tone the P/C chip via toneForDirectionalIntent (bullish→blue, bearish→red), matching MarketScreen's MARKET_BULLISH_TONE treatment, instead of toneForFinancialDelta.

rawConfirmed.issue: The Calendar event-type rail (tc, lines 1240-1247, rendered as the rail background at line 1269) assigns reserved status colors to benign categories: earnings→green (1244), holiday→red (1246), fomc/cpi→amber (1242). Red is reserved for error/loss/danger, so a market holiday reads as a danger state, and green reads as positive P&L — color is used as an arbitrary category key, not meaning.
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: SEMANTIC COLOR — color must describe meaning, not mood/category; red = error/danger, green = positive outcome only.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Use neutral/muted category treatment (e.g., accent or a labeled Badge) for event-type distinction and reserve red/amber/green for actual status, since the event label text (ev.label) already carries the category.

rawConfirmed.issue: The bid/ask spread column colors the ask/call side green via `event.cp === "P" ? CSS_COLOR.red : CSS_COLOR.green` for the 'A {ask}' price label (line 3239) and again in the spread gradient (line 3257). The color keys off call vs put — i.e. it encodes directional option side — so a call renders green.
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: Semantic Color: directional market intent uses blue for call-side/bullish; green is banned for directional intent.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Replace the red/green ternary with toneForOptionSide(event.cp) (already imported at line 45; returns blue for calls, red for puts) at both line 3239 and line 3257.

rawConfirmed.issue: smallButton()'s active state paints GREEN (CSS_COLOR.greenBg background + CSS_COLOR.green text) as a generic 'selected/active' highlight. This green encodes no operational-health, P&L, or directional meaning — it is pure selection emphasis. It is applied to non-health toggles (timeframe favorites toggle 2377-2388, animation/compact/threshold toggles 3057/3076/3092, snooze-clear 2446) and, most contradictorily, to the SignalMonitor Save button when dirty (2807) — where green signals 'go/healthy' even though the same screen correctly flags 'Unsaved' in AMBER (2904) and the top action bar uses accent 'primary' (3250) for the same dirty state.

rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Route selection highlight to accent (as SegmentedControl's indicator does) or a neutral emphasis token; make the dirty Save use accent/primary (matching Button variant='primary') and never CSS_COLOR.green/greenBg for generic active state.

rawConfirmed.issue: SourceBadge is a hand-rolled pill (inline border/background/borderRadius via cssColorAlpha with raw hex-alpha literals "66"/"10", uppercase letter-spaced text) that duplicates the Badge/StatusPill primitive. rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Replace with the Badge or StatusPill primitive from primitives.jsx; drive tone from SEMANTIC_TONE (amber=pending_restart, accent=env, muted=default) instead of inline cssColorAlpha hex strings.

rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: Semantic Color — green is for positive P&L/return/gain; neutral metadata uses muted text/border tones.

rawConfirmed.issue: FooterStatusField collapses operational health to a binary green/red dot; call sites pass ok=Boolean(session.configured.ibkr|research) (lines 1461,1467), so a provider that is simply NOT CONFIGURED is painted RED. Red is reserved for error/unavailable/offline.
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: SEMANTIC COLOR — OPERATIONAL health: GREEN healthy/live/connected, AMBER stale/pending/degraded/limited, RED error/unavailable/blocked/offline. semanticToneModel.js lists not-configured/unconfigured under AMBER_OPERATION_TOKENS.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Drive the dot tone from a 3-state operational status using SEMANTIC_TONE.operationalAttention (amber) for the not-configured state, reserving red for actual failure/offline.

rawConfirmed.issue: IBKR StatusChip renders 'OFF' (session.configured.ibkr === false, i.e. not configured) with tone=CSS_COLOR.red. Not-configured is an attention/limited state, not an error/offline state.
rawConfirmed.doctrine_rule: SEMANTIC COLOR — OPERATIONAL: AMBER stale/pending/degraded/limited vs RED error/unavailable/offline; semanticToneModel.js classifies not-configured as AMBER.
rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Use SEMANTIC_TONE.operationalAttention (CSS_COLOR.amber) for the not-configured 'OFF' state instead of CSS_COLOR.red.

rawConfirmed.issue: A bare FlaskConical lucide icon (size 14, textMuted) sits at the end of the footer row with no onClick, no label, no tooltip, and no status binding — it is purely decorative flourish (a 'lab/experimental' vibe) with no affordance or status meaning, and is not aria-hidden.

rawConfirmed.canonical_fix: Remove the decorative icon, or make it a real status/affordance (labeled control with aria-label/tooltip, e.g. an experimental-mode StatusPill); at minimum mark aria-hidden if it must stay as pure ornament.
